LAW AND MOTION 302, MOTION NOTICE OF SPECIAL MOTION TO STRIKE AND MOTION TO STRIKE PLAINTIFF'S COMPLAINT AND EACH CAUSE OF ACTION THEREIN. RULING: ARGUED, TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED - GRANT SPECIAL MOTION TO STRIKE THE 2ND C/A FOR BREACH OF IMPLIED COVENANT OF GOOD FAITH AND FAIR DEALING; AND THE 8TH C/A FOR VIOLATION OF B&P?17200 BASED ON PETITIONING THE ZONING ADMINISTRATOR. CLAIMS BARRED BY CIVIL?47(B)(3) PRIVILEGE. DENY SPECIAL MOTION TO STRIKE FIRST, THIRD, FOURTH, FIFTH, SIXTH, AND SEVENTH CAUSES OF ACTION. THE COURT FINDS THAT THESE ARE NOT SLAPP CLAIMS. GRANT DEFENDANTS' ATTORNEY FEES AND COSTS. DEFENDANT TO FILE A MEMORANDUM OF COSTS. DENY PLAINTIFF'S REQUEST FOR COSTS, BECAUSE THIS MOTION IS NOT FRIVOLOUS. COURT GRANTS MOTION TO STRIKE ALLEGATIONS IN THE SECOND & EIGHT CAUSE OF ACTION REGARDING DEFTS' CONTACTING EH ZONING ADMINISTRATOR/CITY OFFICIAL. NO DISCOVERY ALLOWED. TEN DAYS TO AMEND COMPLAINT. COUNSEL TO PREP ORDER. COUNSEL TO PREP ORDER. (AJR/CW) REPORTER: LELAND BATARA.(302)

Off Calendar Aug-05-2002 Text Ruling
For Relief From Discovery Stay Pursuant To Code Of Civil Procedure 425.16g

DEMURRER
Off Calendar Jun-19-2002 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCE: DEE A. WARE, 433-1500 FOR PLTF; STEPHEN S. MAYNE, 788-0900 FOR DEFT.
RULING: ARGUED, TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED. SUSTAIN demurrers to Third, Fifth, and Sixth causes of action with leave to amend to allege conduct with specificity.
OVERRULE demurrers to 4th and 7th cause of action, economic loss doctrine does not apply here.
OVERRULE demurrer to specific items in the prayer as procedurally improper.
ON ITS OWN MOTION, the court STRIKES ?13 of prayer seeking Treble Damages because no damages are allowed in private suit to enforce 17200. See Cel-Tech Communications, Inc. v. Los Angeles Cellular Telephone Co. (1999, 20 Cal.4th 163. 10 DAYS TO AMEND. COUNSEL TO SUBMIT ORDER.

MOTION Notice Of Special Motion To Strike And Motion To Strike Plaintiff'S Complaint And Each Cause Of Action Therein
Off Calendar Jun-19-2002 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCE: DEE A. WARE, 433-1500 FOR PLTF; STEPHEN S. MAYNE, 788-0900 FOR DEFT.
RULING: ARGUED, TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED -
GRANT special motion to strike the 2nd c/a for breach of implied covenant of good faith and fair dealing; and the 8th c/a for Violation of B&P?17200 based on petitioning the zoning administrator. Claims barred by Civil?47(b)(3) privilege.
DENY special motion to strike first, third, fourth, fifth, sixth, and seventh causes of action. The court finds that these are not SLAPP claims.
GRANT Defendants' attorney fees and costs. Defendant to file a memorandum of costs.
DENY Plaintiff's request for costs, because this motion is not frivolous.
COURT GRANTS MOTION TO STRIKE ALLEGATIONS IN THE SECOND & EIGHT CAUSE OF ACTION REGARDING DEFTS' CONTACTING EH ZONING ADMINISTRATOR/CITY OFFICIAL. NO DISCOVERY ALLOWED. TEN DAYS TO AMEND COMPLAINT. COUNSEL TO PREP ORDER. COUNSEL TO PREP ORDER.
